name: Senda del Asturcón — circular from Lugo de Llanera
name_original: Senda del Asturcón — circular desde Lugo de Llanera
region_intro: Hiking around Llanera, located in Asturias, Spain, offers access to a diverse landscape. The region features lush countryside and a mountainous interior, including parts of the Cantabrian Mountains. This area is characterized by varied terrain, from gentle river paths to more challenging ascents. The natural features make it suitable for active outdoor experiences. Best hiking trails around Llanera The most popular hiking route is Hiking loop from Posada de Llanera, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the local countryside around Posada de Llanera. Another top favourite among local hikers is Friera Peak loop from Santuyanu, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route features varied terrain and ascends towards a local peak, offering views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the View of Oviedo – View of Oviedo loop from Lugones, a 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through the landscape near Lugones, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Llanera is defined by lush countryside, river valleys, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding ascents. The routes in Llanera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 3,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Llanera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Llanera?
A: Llanera offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 270 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels of hikes in Llanera?
A: The trails around Llanera are categorized into three main difficulty levels: easy, moderate, and difficult. There are approximately 90 easy routes, over 160 moderate routes, and about 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for all experience levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Llanera?
A: Yes, Llanera has several easy routes suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40345651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nora River loop from El Parque&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long and features minimal elevation gain, making it a pleasant walk for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Llanera?
A: Many of the trails in Llanera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Posada de Llanera&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.9 km trail that explores the local countryside.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on hikes in Llanera?
A: Llanera's landscape includes lush countryside, river valleys, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. You can find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peñona&lt;/a&gt;, and explore natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarones of the Tuernes River&lt;/a&gt;. The region offers diverse scenery for hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Llanera?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Llanera, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 380 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from gentle river paths to ascents offering views, and the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails in Llanera?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations vary, many trails in natural areas are generally suitable for dogs on a leash. The region's open countryside and river paths, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Posada de Llanera&lt;/a&gt;, often provide good opportunities for walks with your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Llanera?
A: Llanera, located in Asturias, Spain, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring brings lush greenery, while autumn showcases vibrant colors. Summers are typically warm but can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Llanera area?
A: For those seeking a longer and more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nora River – View of Oviedo loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.6 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a more demanding adventure through the landscape near Lugones.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I visit near Llanera after a hike?
A: Llanera's proximity to Oviedo allows access to significant historical sites. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4395523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of San Salvador in Oviedo&lt;/a&gt; or the pre-Romanesque church of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Mary of Naranco&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a glimpse into the rich cultural heritage of the region.

Q: Are there options for public transport or parking near the trailheads in Llanera?
A: Llanera is a municipality in Asturias, Spain, and generally has good road access. Parking is often available in towns and villages near trailheads. For public transport, local bus services connect various points within the region, though specific routes and schedules should be checked locally depending on your chosen trailhead.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of Oviedo?
A: Yes, some routes provide scenic views towards Oviedo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5341914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Oviedo – View of Oviedo loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.0-mile (12.9 km) trail that leads through the landscape near Lugones, offering vistas of the city.
tips: Lugo de Llanera is a small town known for its beautiful natural surroundings and traditional Asturian architecture. Some of the notable landmarks include the Iglesia de San Miguel, which dates back to the 17th century and features an impressive Baroque altar, and the Palacio de Valdecarzana, a 16th-century Renaissance building that now serves as the town hall. The village is surrounded by beautiful countryside, with rolling hills and lush forests, and the River Nora is a popular spot for fishing and picnicking.

In Llanera there are several archaeological remains from the Lower Paleolithic, which are the oldest located in this council. In Santo firme, La Bérvola and Caraviés several lithic tools have been located, such as bifaces, cleavers, trihedral picks, scrapers, etc. which are the first testimony of the settlement of Llanera.
